entry.title: How to linearize your cluster
entry.summary:
The recent updates to Bitcoin Core demonstrate significant advancements in the project's development, showcasing the integration of Simplified Fee Logic (SFL) into its master branch. This development is marked by the merging of three pivotal pull requests ([PR 32545](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/32545), [PR 34259](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34259), and [PR 34023](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34023)). These contributions are instrumental in streamlining transaction fee calculations, enhancing user experience and system efficiency.
Furthermore, the inclusion of SFL in Bitcoin Core is not merely a developmental milestone; it sets the stage for its deployment in the forthcoming 31.0 release. The anticipation surrounding this update is palpable within the community, as indicated by discussions and documentation available on the official [Bitcoin Core 31.0 release issue page](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues/33607). This new version promises to bring critical improvements to the platform, reinforcing Bitcoin's infrastructure and its commitment to providing secure, reliable digital currency transactions.
